passage: The hiking trail along the river was closed for three months last summer due to flood damage. Crews worked to repair washed-out sections and reinforce eroded banks. The trail [1]_____ to the public in September, just in time for the fall foliage season.

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from a parks department update

stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
explanation: This question tests verb tense with a definite past time marker. 'In September' combined with the past-tense narrative ('was closed,' 'worked') requires simple past. The singular 'trail' takes 'reopened.'

distractorRationale.A: Present singular—conflicts with the past narrative.
distractorRationale.B: Correct. Simple past matching 'in September' and the established past tense.
distractorRationale.C: Base form/plural—wrong tense and disagrees with singular subject.
distractorRationale.D: Present perfect—connects past to present, but the 'last summer' context establishes a purely past narrative.
